title: Planning and Decision-Making / District and Campus Planning
description: This policy governs how the school district creates and approves annual improvement plans, both at the district level and for individual campuses, as required by Texas state law. It applies to the entire school district, including administrators, staff, and the school board, who are all involved in the planning and decision-making process. Parents and community members should care about this policy because the District Improvement Plan sets the direction for student achievement and school programs, and state law requires that community input be part of how these plans are developed.

timeline.summary: The board is asked to approve the 2025-2026 District Improvement Plan (DIP), as required by state law. The DIP is developed annually by the superintendent with the district-level planning and decision-making committee, then presented to the board for adoption. It guides district and campus staff in improving student performance to meet Texas Accountability System standards. This is a routine annual approval, not a change to board policy text itself.
timeline.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline.notes: This agenda item references Board Policy BQ and BQB (Legal) as the authority for the action, but it is not a policy text amendment. It is the annual adoption of the District Improvement Plan as required by state law. Developed and approved by the District Level Planning and Decision-Making Committee prior to board presentation. Submitted by Dr. Ted Landry, Deputy Superintendent.
